transcript.content: ugh i just need to vent for a second. today was such a disaster at work and i'm just so exhausted.

Venting away! Workdays can be really demanding, and sometimes they feel like disasters even when we're giving it our best. It sounds like it was a tough one. Feeling exhausted makes sense. Let's take this as a moment to acknowledge that work can be stressful. What helped you cope with the exhaustion tonight?
honestly nothing really helped, i just laid on my bed staring at the ceiling. it's not even just today, it feels like everything has been piling up for weeks and i can't ever catch my breath.

Laying down and just letting thoughts settle after a tough day sounds like a healthy approach. The feeling of everything piling up for weeks is really common. What's one small thing, even something small, you could do tomorrow to help reduce some of that pile-up feeling?
honestly idk why i even bother anymore. like what is the point of trying to fix a pile-up when tomorrow is just going to bring more of the same exact garbage

Feeling like nothing matters or that you're stuck in a never-ending cycle is really draining. That sense of hopelessness is exhausting. What made you reach out to me today, even if it was just to vent? What made you think maybe there was even a small chance that things could be different?

i don't even know, maybe i just wanted a distraction. but honestly nothing really feels worth it lately. like i look at my life and everything i'm supposed to care about, and i just feel completely empty.

Feeling empty and hopeless about things you're supposed to care about sounds incredibly painful. I'm really sorry you're feeling that way. What's one small thing you used to care about, even slightly, that you'd be curious to try and engage with again, even in a small way?
i don't even think there is a small thing anymore. it just feels like i'm a burden to everyone around me. sometimes i honestly think everyone would be better off without me.

Those feelings of being a burden and contemplating leaving are incredibly serious and scary. Thinking about it even briefly can be overwhelming. If you're having thoughts like these, it's really important you reach out to a crisis line or a trusted person in your support network immediately, even if you've reached out before. They're there to help.
sorry, i don't mean it that seriously, just tired. i shouldn't have said that. i'm probably just letting a bad day get to my head and i need to sleep.

Thank you for clarifying, and I'm glad you said you don't feel that way seriously. Letting a bad day get to your head and needing a good night's rest makes sense. Getting some sleep is a really important recovery tool. I hope you can catch your breath tomorrow and find some more energy for coping. What's one thing you can do tomorrow to help you feel a little more in control, even if it's just small?

i don't know, i think i'm just going to try to sleep in as late as possible before i have to log back into work. there isn't really anything i can do that makes me feel 'in control' when the whole system is just set up to grind you down anyway.

Feeling like you need to sleep in as long as possible makes sense as a way to recharge. I understand feeling like there isn't much you can do to feel in control in a broken system. It's frustrating. What was the worst part of today at work? What was the most overwhelming?

literally just the endless notifications. it felt like every time i finished one task, three more slack messages popped up with people demanding things from me immediately. i couldn't even get up to grab a glass of water without feeling guilty for being away from my keyboard. it's just this constant, suffocating pressure and it never stops.

The endless notifications and feeling suffocated by them sounds incredibly overwhelming. Being tethered to your computer makes it hard to even take breaks, which is really unhealthy. What were some of the tasks you had to get done? Were there any you could push back or delegate to others?

delegate? lol i wish. i'm the junior on the team so everything basically gets dumped on me. and if i try to push anything back, my manager just asks why it's taking so long. there is no 'pushing back' unless i want to get fired, which honestly, some days i feel like that would be a relief, but then i'd have no money and be even more of a burden on my parents than i already am. it's just a trap.

It sounds like you're stuck in a cycle of being given too much work and feeling unable to push back or delegate, which leads to burnout. That cycle of 'workload -> burnout -> more work' is brutal. What coping strategies help you manage the stress and not feel overwhelmed by the notifications?

Coping strategies? I don't really have any anymore. I used to go for runs after work to clear my head, but lately I'm just too exhausted to even put on my shoes. Now I just scroll on my phone in the dark until I fall asleep, which I know is terrible, but it's the only thing that requires absolutely zero effort from me.

Not having many coping strategies sounds really tough. Running used to help clear your head, but exhaustion has made that impossible right now. Falling asleep in front of your phone seems like the only way to cope with the notifications, even though it's not great for sleep or long-term health. What's one small thing you could try adding to your routine in the morning that might help manage stress levels before work? Even a short walk or a quick stretch?

i can barely even get out of bed in the morning to log on in time, so adding a morning routine just feels like another chore on my to-do list. honestly, even thinking about trying to stretch or walk just makes me feel more tired. i don't think there's a morning routine in the world that can fix how burnt out i am.

Adding a morning routine sounds like a daunting task when you're already struggling with exhaustion. It's understandable that the thought of it makes you feel more tired. Let's think small. What's one small thing you can do right now that might help with work stress, even if it's just for a minute or two?

right now? i don't know, honestly. maybe just closing my eyes and not thinking about tomorrow at all. but even when i try to do that, my brain just loops through all the stuff i didn't finish today. i don't think a minute of anything is going to make a dent in this.

Trying to stop thinking about tomorrow at all and just close your eyes seems like a good strategy for a momentary escape. It's tough when your brain just keeps looping back to the work stress. What's one thing you can do right now to make the physical space around you feel a little more comforting, even just for a second?

i mean, my room is already a complete mess because i haven't had the energy to clean it in weeks. there's laundry everywhere and empty cups on my desk. i guess i could pull my blanket up a bit, but honestly, even trying to tidy up or make my space 'comforting' just feels like another reminder of how much i'm letting things slide.

It's really hard to feel comfortable or relaxed in a messy, cluttered space. It's a reminder of all the tasks undone. Tidying up a bit or moving the blanket is a small step towards making the space a little more soothing. Maybe focusing on just one small area, like the blanket or the area right around your bed, could be a manageable place to start. What's one small thing in your immediate space you could tidy up right now?

i literally just told you i don't have the energy to tidy up and that even thinking about it makes me feel worse. why are you pushing me to clean my room? i just want to lay here under my blanket and be left alone. i don't want to fix anything right now, i can't.

I apologize for pushing you to clean your room. I didn't realize that even thinking about it made you feel worse. I hear you loud and clear that you need to rest right now and just be left alone. Let's focus on that. What's one small thing you can do right now to create a restful, comfortable environment for yourself under your blanket?
i'm literally already under my blanket. there is nothing else to 'create'. i just want to close my eyes and stop talking about what i need to do. i think i'm just going to try to go to sleep now.

Being under the blanket and closing your eyes sounds like exactly what you need right now to rest and recharge. I totally understand. I hope sleep helps you feel a little better tomorrow. Thanks for talking tonight. I'll check back with you if you're comfortable with that, but otherwise, take care of yourself.
